name: 26_Mushroom_Classification_RandomForest_Mushroom_ML
query: Develop a mushroom classification system using a Random Forest model on the UCI Mushroom dataset. Load the dataset in the `src/data_loader.py` file. Ensure that feature engineering, including feature encoding and feature selection, and missing data handling are completed in `src/data_loader.py` before training the model. Train the Random Forest classifier on the processed dataset in `src/train.py`. Save the classification results `results/classification_results.txt`. Visualize and save the feature importance as `results/figures/feature_importance.png`, ensuring the visualization clearly highlights the most influential features. Create an interactive web page in `src/app.py` using Streamlit to showcase the classification results and model performance. The Streamlit web page should provide an overview of the model's performance and allow users to interact with the classification results. The system should manages the start and end of the Streamlit visualization properly.

requirements.criteria: The "UCI Mushroom" dataset is loaded in the `src/data_loader.py` file.
Feature engineering is performed, including feature encoding and feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`.
Missing data is handled to ensure the dataset is clean before training in `src/data_loader.py`.
A Random Forest classifier is trained on the processed dataset in `src/train.py`.
The classification results are saved in `results/classification_results.txt`.
Feature importance is visualized and saved as `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.
An interactive web page is created in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" to showcase classification results and model performance.

preferences.criteria: The feature importance visualization should clearly highlight the most influential features, making it easy to interpret.
The Streamlit web page should provide an overview of the model's performance and allow users to interact with the classification results.
The system properly manages the start and end of the Streamlit visualization .

judge_stats.criteria: The "UCI Mushroom" dataset is loaded in the `src/data_loader.py` file.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually load the UCI Mushroom dataset. Instead, it contains a minimal placeholder/stub implementation. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual data loading logic. There is no code to fetch, read, or parse the UCI Mushroom dataset (e.g., no `pandas.read_csv()`, no UCI repository download, no file path referencing mushroom data). The file merely lists "UCI Mushroom" as a keyword string in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS` lists, which is not equivalent to actually loading the dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature engineering is performed, including feature encoding and feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a minimal placeholder/stub implementation. It does not perform any actual feature engineering. The file contains:

1. A `load_dataset()` function that simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual data loading.
2. A `build_transforms()` function that returns a static list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` — no actual encoding or selection logic.

There is no implementation of feature encoding (e.g., label encoding, one-hot encoding) or feature selection (e.g., selecting relevant columns, dropping irrelevant features) anywhere in the file. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a functional implementation. The requirement for actual feature engineering operations in `src/data_loader.py` is not met.

judge_stats.criteria: Missing data is handled to ensure the dataset is clean before training in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a minimal placeholder implementation. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual data loading, and there is no missing data handling whatsoever (no `dropna()`, `fillna()`, imputation, or any other data cleaning operations). The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and does not perform any real data processing.

judge_stats.criteria: A Random Forest classifier is trained on the processed dataset in `src/train.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file does not implement a Random Forest classifier. Instead, it contains a "minimal semantic implementation" that only performs a trivial loop (`score += step`) with no machine learning functionality. There is no import of `sklearn.ensemble.RandomForestClassifier` or any other ML library, no dataset loading, no model training, and no actual classification logic. The file merely creates placeholder artifact files (`classification_results.txt` and `feature_importance.png`) with dummy text content. The requirement to train a Random Forest classifier on the processed dataset is not fulfilled.

judge_stats.criteria: The classification results are saved in `results/classification_results.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/classification_results.txt` exists, but its content is merely a placeholder string: `('placeholder\r\n', None)`. This indicates the file was not properly populated with actual classification results (e.g., accuracy metrics, confusion matrix, classification report). The content suggests the code either failed to execute the classification pipeline or wrote a default/fallback value instead of real results.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature importance is visualized and saved as `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/feature_importance.png` exists in the project structure, as confirmed by the workspace tree which explicitly lists `results\figures\feature_importance.png`. The file is present at the expected path, indicating that the feature importance visualization was generated and saved. The error shown in the file content is a reading/display error from the evaluation tool (related to image format detection during base64 encoding), not an indication that the file is missing or corrupt — the file itself exists as a PNG image at the correct location.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web page is created in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" to showcase classification results and model performance.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/app.py` file does not contain a Streamlit-based interactive web page. Instead, it contains a minimal stub/placeholder implementation that only defines requirement criteria strings and a `main()` function returning a status dictionary. There is no Streamlit import (`import streamlit as st`), no Streamlit UI components (e.g., `st.title()`, `st.write()`, `st.dataframe()`, charts, etc.), and no actual functionality to showcase classification results or model performance. The file is essentially a meta-description of the requirement rather than an implementation of it.
